UNH.reasoning: All 5 strategy agents decline to take a position on UNH today, though for different reasons that collectively paint a coherent picture: the stock is in a historically extreme parabolic extension that is not yet broken but is not actionable either. Vote count: 0 bullish / 1 bearish / 4 neutral. The lone directional call comes from swing_mean_reversion, which sees a fade opportunity (RSI-14 97.45, RSI-7 98.07, z-score 2.34, price 23.85% above 50 SMA) but caps confidence at 42% because ADX-84 signals a runaway trend and no confirmed reversal candle exists. The other four agents are unanimously neutral for overlapping but distinct reasons: swing_trend_momentum requires a pullback to the 10 EMA ($352–358) before entering long — price is $18 above that zone; swing_breakout finds no coiling range, no squeeze, and 0.74x volume — a clean no-setup verdict; swing_catalyst_news identifies no binary within 10 trading days, Q1 earnings are fully absorbed, and the RICO insulin-pricing lawsuit is a new unpriced legal tail that adds noise not conviction; swing_macro_context confirms risk-on regime is broadly supportive but applies a capital-preservation veto — RSI-97 is a 5-sigma reading, R/R to Bollinger upper ($385.50) is only 1.8:1 (below the 2:1 minimum), and the NVDA lesson (RSI 81-87 at entry, stop triggered intraday) is directly analogous. Wiki memory adds important nuance: yesterday's run (Apr 30) explicitly said 'hold — wait for pullback to $348–355,' and the two prior UNH positions (trades 1 and 9) were limit buy orders that never filled because the system refused to chase. Today's price is even further extended from those entry levels. No pattern failure from lessons applies to UNH today (the only lesson is an EMA dip-buy LONG setup that stopped out, which is structurally different from a neutral hold). Consensus is emphatically neutral: pass until either (a) a pullback to the $350–358 10-EMA zone materializes with hourly momentum realigning bullish, or (b) a confirmed daily reversal candle prints below $368 with hourly non-confirmation, enabling a mean-reversion short with defined risk above $372.
UNH.key_conflicts: The sole conflict is swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 42%) versus the four neutral agents. Mean_reversion correctly identifies that UNH is at extreme statistical extension (RSI 97, z-score 2.34, 24% above 50 SMA) and a fade setup technically fires — but it tempers conviction sharply because ADX-84 is the most powerful counter-signal: runaway trends can stay overbought far longer than short-sellers expect, and no confirmed reversal candle exists. Swing_trend_momentum is not bullish here — it agrees price is extended and explicitly requires a pullback entry, making it functionally neutral rather than a bullish counterweight. The macro context agent underscores the asymmetry problem: upside to the Bollinger upper band is only +4.1% while downside to the 10 EMA is -4.9%, producing a 1.8:1 R/R that fails the 2:1 minimum for a long. The disagreement between mean_reversion (wants to fade) and trend_momentum (wants to buy the dip when it comes) is telling: both agents are waiting, just for different triggers. The RICO legal overhang flagged by catalyst_news is an unpriced tail that macro_context does not offset — it is an additional reason not to chase. Net read: the disagreement does not produce a directional trade; it produces a disciplined hold.

JNJ.reasoning: Four of five agents are directionally bearish on JNJ, with three of those (swing_trend_momentum, swing_mean_reversion, swing_breakout) fully aligned on the short thesis and swing_macro_context adding a regime-level bearish overlay. Only swing_catalyst_news is neutral. Vote count: 4 bearish / 1 neutral / 0 bullish. The consensus bearish case is well-integrated: (1) The daily trend is confirmed — EMAs fully inverted (EMA10 $229.64 < EMA21 $232.66 < EMA50 $233.78), ADX 30.72 with -DI 29.6 dominating +DI 19.76, MACD line -3.23 expanding below signal -2.67, OBV trending down, 10-day volume bias down. (2) Price is in a 2.2% counter-trend bounce from the $224.86 low (Apr 29) to $229.85 (Apr 30), currently testing the 10-EMA resistance zone at $229–231 — the textbook short entry point in a downtrend. (3) Hourly Bollinger Band width is 0.0289 (extremely tight squeeze), confirming the bounce is coiling, not breaking out to the upside. (4) The macro regime — S&P 500 at ATH, risk-on rotation — is a direct headwind for defensive healthcare; capital is rotating out of JNJ into growth and cyclicals. (5) R/R from the current entry zone is approximately 4.3:1 (downside to Fib extension $217.41 vs risk to $232.73 stop), well above the 3:1 preferred threshold. Wiki memory is a strong confirmation dial: the Apr 30 run placed an active short at $229.50, target $216.53, stop $235 — this run's signals are a continuation of that thesis, not a new setup. The prior two hold decisions (Apr 11, Apr 15) were correctly patient; the squeeze resolved bearish. Confidence is dialed down from the raw vote to 58% for three reasons: (a) an open short position already exists from Apr 30 ($229.50 entry) — capital is already deployed at essentially the same price, so adding here means pyramiding, not opening fresh; (b) daily RSI at 36.5 is approaching oversold territory (not yet at the extreme <25 level, but declining), which increases the chance of a deeper counter-bounce before the next leg down; (c) swing_catalyst_news correctly notes that no upcoming scheduled catalyst supports the short — the thesis relies on continuing technical deterioration rather than a hard binary catalyst. The lessons pattern table shows only one trade failure — an EMA dip-buy long that stopped out — which does not apply to this bearish setup. No pattern-level confidence dial-down from lessons.
JNJ.key_conflicts: The only meaningful conflict is swing_catalyst_news (neutral, 78%) against the four bearish agents. Catalyst_news correctly identifies that there is no scheduled catalyst within 10 trading days to trigger the next leg down — the bearish trade relies purely on technical deterioration and macro rotation rather than a hard binary event. This is not a fatal objection (most swing trades are technically-driven) but it is a legitimate confidence cap: without a catalyst, the downtrend can stall or produce a larger counter-bounce before resuming. The agent also notes that daily RSI at 36.5 is approaching oversold, and the IMAAVY FDA Priority Review is a latent positive pipeline signal, both of which could produce a short-term relief rally. The three technical agents (trend_momentum, mean_reversion, breakout) all agree on the short entry zone ($229–231) and the downside target ($216–217 Fib extension), which reduces level conflict. The only level disagreement is the stop: trend_momentum uses $235, mean_reversion uses $233, breakout uses $235, and macro_context uses $232.73. The cluster median is approximately $233–$235, so $235 (the widest stop, also used in the active Apr 30 trade) is the appropriate stop level — it clears the EMA21 resistance zone and aligns with the existing open trade's stop. The macro veto question: macro_context is bearish at 65% and confirms the regime headwind; it does not veto the short thesis but strengthens it. No macro-bullish veto scenario applies here. Net: the conflict between catalyst_news (neutral) and the four bearish agents is a confidence dial-down, not a directional override. An open short already exists from yesterday at essentially the same entry — adding requires justification for pyramiding into an oversold-approaching setup.

UNH.reasoning: MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with near-term caveats. S&P 500 at all-time highs above 7,200 on May 1 (+0.54%), driven by Apple earnings beat and Iran peace talks relief. Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (third consecutive hold, 8-4 vote). Risk-on regime favors growth/tech rotation, not defensive healthcare — a macro headwind for UNH even as it holds momentum. RELATIVE STRENGTH: UNH is a massive outperformer on a trailing basis — ROC 21d at +36.92%, outperforming the broad market by a wide margin. However, this is a LATE-cycle setup flag, not a bullish entry signal. The wiki thesis explicitly states: 'extended momentum leader at statistical extremes — wait for pullback to $348-355 EMA zone before re-entering long.' RSI 14 at 97.45 and RSI 21 at 94.55 are statistical anomalies. ADX at 84.15 confirms extreme trend strength but also signals exhaustion risk. Z-score 50 at 2.34 = price is 2.34 standard deviations above its 50-day mean. These are historically mean-reverting levels. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Upside to Fib ext 1.272 at $403.55 = +8.9% from current $370.48. Downside to EMA 10 zone ($352) = -4.9%. R/R approximately 1.8:1 — BELOW the 2:1 minimum threshold. Hourly MACD histogram is slightly negative (-1.01), suggesting near-term momentum is fading. Bollinger %B at 0.87 puts price near the upper band ($385.50); upper band is next resistance. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: RSI at 97+ is a 5-standard-deviation reading. Mean reversion from this level historically produces sharp -15% to -25% corrections. The prior NVDA lesson (RSI 81-87 at entry, stop triggered intraday) is directly analogous — same macro regime, same stretched RSI condition, same late-entry pattern. SECTOR CYCLE TIMING: UNH surged +41.6% in 21 trading days post-Q1 earnings (April 21). This is the late phase of a sector move, not the early phase. Bullish entries should come early in a relative-strength uptrend; this is end-stage. VERDICT: The macro regime is risk-on (supportive in general) but the stock is at extreme statistical extension (RSI 97, ADX 84, z-score 2.34, 24% above 50 SMA), R/R is below threshold at 1.8:1, and the wiki memory explicitly marks this as 'wait for pullback.' Neutral — pass until $348-355 EMA pullback zone is reached.

JNJ.reasoning: MACRO REGIME: Risk-on environment with S&P 500 at all-time highs above 7,200 on May 1. This is a direct headwind for defensive healthcare names like JNJ — in a strong risk-on regime, capital rotates away from defensives (healthcare, utilities, staples) into growth and cyclicals. Iran peace talks reducing energy-driven inflation fears further diminish the defensive flight-to-safety bid that would normally support JNJ's dividend yield. Fed on hold at 3.5-3.75% means no near-term rate cuts, reducing the appeal of JNJ's dividend relative to fixed income alternatives. RELATIVE STRENGTH: JNJ is materially underperforming the broad market. ROC 21d at -5.97% vs S&P 500's best month since 2020. ROC 10d: -2.0%. Z-score 50 at -1.45 (below mean). EMAs aligned in confirmed downtrend (ema_aligned_downtrend: true on both daily and hourly). Price is below EMA 20, EMA 21, EMA 50 on the daily — classic distribution pattern. Daily ADX at 30.72 with minus_di (29.6) > plus_di (19.76) confirms a strong bearish trend is in force. Volume bias 10d: down; OBV trend: down — institutional distribution confirmed. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry near current price $229.85 (or slight bounce to $230-231 resistance). Stop above EMA 20 / Fib 61.8% zone at $232.73. Risk: $232.73 - $229.85 = $2.88 (1.25%). Primary target: break below $223.78 recent low → Fib extension low 1.272 at $217.41. Downside: $229.85 - $217.41 = $12.44 (5.41%). R/R: 5.41% / 1.25% = 4.3:1 — well above the 3:1 preferred threshold. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: JNJ has a strong balance sheet (market cap $548B, well-capitalized pharma/medtech franchise) — no balance-sheet leverage concern. Volatility ATR 2.09% daily is moderate, consistent with the regime. No earnings imminent flagged. SECTOR CYCLE TIMING: JNJ's downtrend began from ~$247 and price has fallen to $229 — mid-cycle of the decline, not extended. The wiki thesis confirms: 'JNJ is now a short candidate in a confirmed daily downtrend' following the bearish squeeze resolution. The setup is in the middle of a sector rotation away from defensive healthcare, not late stage. HOURLY CONTEXT: Hourly EMAs aligned downtrend. Price below all hourly MAs. Volume relative is 0.14 (very low) — the Apr 30 bounce to $229.85 is low-conviction. Hourly RSI 21 at 50.43 shows the bounce is merely mean reversion, not a trend change. Hourly MACD histogram is +0.32 — slight bullish tilt on the hourly suggesting a short-term consolidation/bounce, making this a good entry zone for the bearish swing rather than chasing the downside immediately. VERDICT: Bearish. Risk-on macro removes the defensive bid, confirmed downtrend on daily with strong ADX bearish configuration, institutional distribution via OBV, and 4.3:1 R/R to Fib extension target. Wiki memory aligns. Enter on current price or slight bounce to $230-231 resistance zone.

UNH.reasoning: Branch A fires but with extreme caution. Daily RSI-14 at 97.45, RSI-7 at 98.07, and RSI-21 at 94.55 are all at historic extremes (threshold >75). Z-score vs 50-day SMA is +2.34 (threshold >2.0 for overbought). Price is 23.85% above the 50-SMA (extreme: >10%). These are textbook Branch A fade conditions. HOWEVER: ADX-14 at 84.15 signals a runaway trend — the strongest possible counter-signal for a fade. The daily Bollinger pct_b is 0.869, still inside the upper band (upper at $385.50), meaning price has not yet pierced the upper band. Critically, the hourly RSI is only 56.41 — not overbought — and hourly z-score is 1.33 (below the 2.0 threshold). The divergence between daily extreme and hourly neutral means the reversal has NOT yet started on shorter timeframes. No confirmed reversal candle: the last four sessions show continuation ($354.92 → $354.69 → $366.77 → $370.74 → $370.48), with Apr 30 closing nearly flat ($370.48 vs $370.74 prev close) on declining volume (6.88M vs 20-day avg 9.31M — rel vol 0.74). This flat-top candle on declining volume is the first hint of exhaustion but is NOT yet a confirmed reversal. Wiki confirms: yesterday's run flagged RSI 94.3 / z-score 2.54 / ADX 81.64 and explicitly said 'wait for pullback to $348–355 EMA zone.' Today's read is marginally less extreme (z-score 2.34 vs 2.54) but essentially unchanged. Branch B (dip buy): not applicable — price is at recent highs, no pullback setup. ENTRY: on a confirmed bearish reversal candle below $368 (prior session low). Stop above the recent high of $371.99. Target: 10-EMA at $352.17 (partial mean reversion, realistic for 2-20 day window). Full mean reversion to 50-SMA ($299) is not a 20-day target given the runaway trend. Confidence capped at 42 due to ADX=84 extreme trend strength, lack of confirmed reversal candle, and hourly non-confirmation.

JNJ.reasoning: Branch A partial + Branch B downtrend short-the-rally setup. Branch A: Daily RSI-14 at 36.5 and RSI-21 at 33.3 approach oversold territory but have NOT breached the <25 extreme threshold. Z-score vs 50-day at -1.45 (threshold needed: <-2.0). Daily Bollinger pct_b at 0.3301 — below the middle band but well above the lower band ($221.75). Branch A does not fully fire for a long fade. Branch B (downtrend): EMAs are fully aligned downtrend (ema_aligned_downtrend: true), ADX-14 at 30.72 with -DI 29.6 > +DI 19.76 confirming a downtrend. MACD line at -3.23 (bearish). OBV trend down. The dominant trend is bearish. Price has bounced from the $224.86 low (Apr 29) to close at $229.85 (Apr 30), a ~2.2% rally. This bounce is testing the 10-EMA resistance zone ($229.64). Notably, the daily RSI-7 is at 62.05 — overbought for a bounce within a downtrend (threshold >65 in a downtrend rally). The hourly Fib 38.2% retracement (from hourly swing high $231.16 to low $224.66) is at $228.68, and current hourly price is $227.93, just at/below this level. The hourly Fib confluence with the nearest hourly resistance at $231.03 provides a resistance zone for a short entry. Hourly MACD histogram is +0.32 (bounce still slightly alive), hourly RSI at 50.43 (neutral) — momentum is fading. Hourly bullish AND bearish divergence both flagged simultaneously (likely noise, but no clean bullish divergence to counter the downtrend). Volume on the bounce: Apr 30 daily volume 8.16M is only slightly above the 20-day avg (7.7M, rel vol 1.06) — not a high-conviction reversal volume surge. Volume bias 10-day is 'down.' Wiki strongly confirms: prior run (Apr 30) issued SHORT at $229.50 (bounce into 10-EMA resistance), target $216.53, stop $235. The Apr 15 bearish trigger ($233 close) was surpassed and the squeeze resolved bearish. This is not a repeat failed attempt — it is a continuation of the confirmed breakdown. Lesson check: the only loss in the pattern table was 'EMA pullback Fib dip-buy' — a LONG setup, not a short. No pattern failure applies to this bearish setup. Entry: $229.50–$230.00 (the 10-EMA resistance zone, aligned with wiki's prior recommendation). Stop: $232.73 (daily Fib 61.8% = structural break level, close to wiki's $235 zone). Target: $223.78 (nearest daily pivot support / prior low), with extension to $221.75 (lower Bollinger). Confidence 62: downtrend is confirmed, wiki aligns, but daily RSI-14 at 36.5 is approaching oversold (not yet extreme), and if the bounce extends further into $231+ the short entry improves. No confirmed daily reversal candle from this bounce yet — the Apr 30 close at $229.85 near the high of day ($231.16) leaves room for one more day of consolidation.
